heaviest_rainfall.status: The most intense rainfall recorded took place on 09 November 2025, with precipitation totalling 47.89mm.  The rain fell at 24.6mm per hour at times over the course of the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning strikes in one day since 11 April 2025 is 23 and it happened on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: No storm activity at the moment.  The previous storm occurred on 17 April 2026 and lasted 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: This period, starting 18 April 2026, is ranked 2nd among the longest dry periods since 23 September 2024.  It has lasted 14 days so far.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 3 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 27.7°C, observed on 24 April 2026 when the temperature fell to -4.5°C but also managed to reach 23.2°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwaves right now.  There have been 0 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.9.

tropical.status: The most recent tropical night was on 18 July 2025 and this has only occurred 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (213°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 128.2 mph from 27 January 2026.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind speed recorded on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 17%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 2182 m) and light levels (up 6814 lux).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 1020 m) and outside temperature (down 1.9°C) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 2180m lower than yesterday, 1020.5m lower than the past week, 107.0m higher than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 2390m to a maximum of 8840m, averaging 2910m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 3930m and 2810m respectively.
cloud levels.summary: Clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; the temperature will feel pleasantly warm. Also, air will feel comfortable and balanced and near calm conditions. Showers, clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight, and moderate levels of sunlight will be present. Weather patterns to stay the same.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
